projectTitle: OmniCommerce Enterprise: Next-Generation Global E-Commerce Platform
executiveSummary: OmniCommerce Enterprise represents a paradigm shift in digital retail, delivering a hyper-personalized, headless commerce experience designed for global scale. By leveraging advanced machine learning algorithms for real-time product recommendations and dynamic pricing, the platform ensures maximum conversion rates and customer lifetime value. The architecture decouples the frontend presentation layer from the robust backend microservices, enabling seamless omnichannel integrations across web, mobile, AR/VR storefronts, and IoT devices. This flagship rollout will empower merchants with a comprehensive suite of tools including a complex data dashboard for predictive inventory management, an immersive interactive hero section for brand storytelling, and frictionless one-click checkout flows. Ultimately, OmniCommerce Enterprise will redefine the digital shopping journey, providing unparalleled performance, enterprise-grade security, and a highly modular ecosystem that adapts instantly to shifting market demands and consumer behaviors.

userStories.story: As a shopper, I want to interact with a 3D immersive hero section so that I can explore flagship products in a 360-degree view before navigating the site.description:The immersive hero section serves as the primary engagement touchpoint upon landing. It utilizes WebGL and Three.js to render high-fidelity 3D models of flagship products, allowing users to interact with the product physically in a digital space, thereby increasing time-on-site and initial engagement metrics.acceptanceCriteria:[Renders 3D model at 60fps on modern desktop and mobile devices.,Includes drag-to-rotate, pinch-to-zoom, and double-tap-to-reset gesture controls.,Gracefully degrades to a high-resolution looping video on low-end devices or unsupported browsers.,Call-to-action buttons overlaying the hero section dynamically adapt their color palette based on the dominant color of the currently viewed 3D model.]

As a returning customer, I want to see a personalized product grid based on my browsing history and purchase behavior so that I can quickly find items relevant to my interests.description:This feature leverages a machine learning recommendation engine to populate a dynamic grid of 12 products tailored specifically to the authenticated user. It aims to reduce friction in the discovery phase and significantly boost the average order value (AOV).acceptanceCriteria:[The recommendation grid updates asynchronously within 200ms of the initial page load.,Displays a 'Why we recommend this' tooltip when a user hovers over a product card.,Supports infinite scrolling with lazy loading of high-resolution WebP images to preserve bandwidth.,Falls back to a 'Trending Now' generic grid if the user is anonymous or lacks sufficient browsing history.]

As a ready-to-buy user, I want a single-page, dynamically validating checkout form with digital wallet integration so that I can complete my purchase in under 30 seconds.description:The checkout flow is the most critical conversion funnel. This story defines a frictionless, single-page application (SPA) checkout experience that minimizes cart abandonment through inline validation, address auto-completion, and one-click payment methods.acceptanceCriteria:[Form fields validate inline using regex for email, phone number, and postal code formats as the user types.,Integrates Stripe Elements for secure, PCI-compliant credit card entry without redirecting the user.,Automatically displays Apple Pay or Google Pay express checkout buttons if supported by the user device and browser.,Calculates tax and shipping costs dynamically via API based on the postal code entry before the final submission button is enabled.]

As a supply chain manager, I want a complex data dashboard displaying predictive inventory alerts and real-time stock levels so that I can prevent stockouts during high-demand periods.description:An enterprise-grade administrative dashboard utilizing D3.js or Recharts for advanced data visualization. It aggregates global inventory data and uses predictive analytics to forecast stock depletion rates, enabling proactive supply chain management.acceptanceCriteria:[Displays a geographical heat map of low-stock items across all registered global warehouses.,Generates automated purchase order drafts when a product stock falls below the dynamically calculated safety threshold.,Allows exporting of inventory forecast reports to PDF and CSV formats with custom date ranges.,Updates dashboard data via WebSockets to ensure real-time accuracy without requiring manual page refreshes.]

As a merchandising manager, I want to configure rules for dynamic pricing based on real-time demand, competitor pricing, and inventory levels so that I can maximize profit margins automatically.description:This story outlines the interface and logic for a dynamic pricing engine. Merchandisers can build complex algorithms using a visual interface to adjust prices on the fly, responding to market conditions instantly while protecting baseline margins.acceptanceCriteria:[Provides a visual rule builder utilizing IF/THEN logic blocks for creating pricing adjustments.,Enforces mandatory minimum price floors to ensure algorithmic changes do not result in negative profit margins.,Includes a simulation mode allowing managers to forecast the potential revenue impact of a new pricing rule against historical data.,Maintains a strict audit log tracking which user created, modified, or deleted specific pricing rules and at what timestamp.]

As a shopper with specific needs, I want to use an AI-powered semantic search bar with multi-faceted filtering so that I can pinpoint exact products from a massive catalog.description:Implementing an Elasticsearch-backed semantic search that understands natural language queries, synonyms, and typos. Coupled with a dynamic faceted sidebar, this allows users to drill down into a catalog of millions of SKUs instantly.acceptanceCriteria:[Handles typographical errors and natural language queries (e.g., 'red running shoes under 50 dollars') returning accurate results.,Faceted filters (price, color, size, rating, availability) update search results instantly without a full page reload.,Search bar provides rich auto-complete suggestions, including category links and product thumbnails, after 3 characters are typed.,Maintains full WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility, including ARIA labels and keyboard navigation support for screen readers.]

As a wholesale buyer, I want to upload a CSV or use a rapid-entry matrix form to add hundreds of SKUs to my cart simultaneously so that I can streamline my procurement process.description:A B2B-specific feature designed for high-volume corporate clients. It bypasses the traditional single-item add-to-cart flow, providing bulk upload capabilities and a spreadsheet-like interface for rapid order construction.acceptanceCriteria:[Parses uploaded CSV files containing up to 10,000 rows within 5 seconds, mapping columns to SKU and Quantity.,Highlights invalid SKUs, discontinued items, or out-of-stock quantities in red with actionable error messages.,Allows inline editing of quantities directly within the matrix UI prior to adding the batch to the cart.,Applies volume-based tier discounts automatically to the subtotal based on the aggregate quantity of the bulk order.]

As a registered user, I want to view my loyalty tier, available points, and exclusive rewards in a dedicated dashboard so that I am incentivized to make repeat purchases.description:A gamified customer portal that visualizes the user loyalty journey. It integrates with the CRM to display points balances, tier progression, and unlockable rewards, fostering brand loyalty and increasing customer lifetime value.acceptanceCriteria:[Displays a visual progress bar indicating the spend required to reach the next loyalty tier (e.g., Silver to Gold).,Allows users to redeem accumulated points for unique discount codes or free shipping vouchers via an interactive modal.,Shows a paginated historical ledger of all points earned from purchases and points spent on rewards.,Triggers a celebratory CSS/canvas confetti animation when a user successfully upgrades to a new loyalty tier.]

As a customer awaiting delivery, I want to view a live interactive map and status timeline of my shipment so that I know exactly when my package will arrive.description:Enhancing the post-purchase experience by providing granular, real-time visibility into order fulfillment and shipping. This reduces customer support inquiries regarding order status and improves overall satisfaction.acceptanceCriteria:[Integrates with major carrier APIs (FedEx, UPS, USPS) via webhooks to receive real-time status updates.,Renders a map interface displaying the delivery vehicle current location if supported by the carrier last-mile tracking API.,Allows users to opt-in to SMS or WhatsApp notifications for critical states: 'Out for Delivery' and 'Delivered'.,Provides a contextual one-click 'Report an Issue' button that routes directly to support if a package is marked delayed.]

As a system administrator, I want to assign granular permissions to staff members so that they can only access the modules necessary for their job functions.description:Establishing a robust Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) system for the enterprise backend. This ensures security, compliance, and operational efficiency by restricting sensitive data and destructive actions to authorized personnel only.acceptanceCriteria:[Supports Single Sign-On (SSO) integration via enterprise identity providers like Okta or Azure Active Directory.,Enforces Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all administrative logins, utilizing authenticator apps or SMS.,Dynamically restricts UI rendering and API access based on the specific claims embedded in the user JWT.,Logs all administrative actions (e.g., issuing refunds, changing inventory) to a secure, immutable audit trail database.]

scope: In-Scope: Development of a headless progressive web application (PWA) storefront, immersive 3D product visualization hero sections, AI-driven recommendation grids, multi-step B2B/B2C checkout flows, dynamic pricing engines, predictive inventory dashboards, role-based admin portals, and integration with top-tier payment gateways (Stripe, PayPal) and ERP systems (SAP, Oracle). Out-of-Scope: Physical warehouse robotics integration, native iOS/Android application development (deferred to Phase 2), legacy on-premise database migration, and third-party vendor marketplace onboarding.
technicalConstraints: Must achieve a Google Lighthouse performance score of 95+ across all core web vitals on both mobile and desktop environments.
Backend microservices must be deployed on Kubernetes clusters with auto-scaling capabilities to handle up to 100,000 concurrent users during flash sales.
Strict adherence to PCI-DSS Level 1 compliance for all payment processing and GDPR/CCPA compliance for user data privacy and consent management.
Frontend must be built using Next.js and React, utilizing Server-Side Rendering (SSR) and Static Site Generation (SSG) for optimal SEO and initial load times.
API response times must not exceed 100ms at the 95th percentile under normal load conditions, utilizing Redis caching where appropriate.
dataEntities: UserAccount (UUID, Email, PasswordHash, Role, Preferences, OrderHistory, LoyaltyPoints, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt)
Product (SKU, Name, Description, CategoryID, BasePrice, DynamicPrice, InventoryLevel, 3DModelURL, Metadata, Status)
Order (OrderID, UserID, TotalAmount, Tax, Shipping, Status, PaymentIntentID, TrackingNumber, Timestamp)
Cart (CartID, SessionID, UserID, ItemsArray, Subtotal, AppliedDiscounts, ExpirationDate)
Inventory (WarehouseID, SKU, QuantityAvailable, ReservedQuantity, RestockDate, SupplierID)
Promotion (PromoCode, DiscountType, Value, EligibilityRules, StartDate, EndDate, UsageLimit)
